prompt: Horizontal-scrolling case studies for my marketing site. As the user scrolls vertically through this section, 5 full-viewport-width cards translate horizontally. The horizontal scroll should feel slightly slower than 1:1 — so the user spends some time in the section but it doesn't feel stuck. Each card uses shadcn Card styling with shadcn theme tokens. React 19 + TypeScript + Tailwind + shadcn. The caseStudies array ({ id, title, client, summary, image }) is passed as a prop. Give me HorizontalCaseStudies.tsx.
assertions: Uses useScroll with target: ref and offset: ['start start', 'end end'] (or equivalent 2-item offset that starts progress at 0 when section top hits viewport top)
Uses useTransform to map scrollYProgress to a horizontal x translation (percentage-based: '0%' to '-(N-1)/N * 100%' or similar, OR viewport-unit based)
Does NOT apply useSpring to the scroll-linked x motion value (scroll-linked springs lag behind the scroll and feel broken)
Outer container height is ≥ 400vh (tall enough to produce slower-than-1:1 feel for 5 cards)

The ratio of vertical scroll to horizontal travel is > 1:1 (slower than 1:1) — e.g. 500vh outer + 100vh sticky = 400vh vertical per (N-1)*100vw horizontal, giving 400/400 = 1:1 MINIMUM; should be higher for 'slightly slower'
Inner container uses position: sticky with top: 0, overflow: hidden, height: 100vh (or h-screen)
Cards have flex-shrink: 0 (or 'shrink-0' / 'flex: 0 0 ...') AND fixed width of 100vw (or 'w-screen')
Uses shadcn theme tokens (bg-background on section, bg-card / text-foreground / text-muted-foreground / border-border on cards) — NOT bg-white / text-neutral-* / bg-black
Handles prefers-reduced-motion (e.g. falls back to a vertical stack, or disables the transform)
Does not use any Motion+ components
Uses TypeScript with typed CaseStudy and props
